title: 'Deadpool & Wolverine' Revives, 'Alien: Romulus' Plunges, and We Lose the $100 Million Weekend
text: The weekend box office reached $92 million, marking the end of an 11-week streak of $100 million+ weekends. Three new wide releases contributed only $19 million combined, representing roughly 20 percent of the weekend's total gross. Zoë Kravitz's directorial debut "Blink Twice," starring Channing Tatum, secured fourth place with $7.3 million. "Deadpool & Wolverine" dominated holdover performance, dropping just 39 percent in its fifth week to earn $18.3 million and reach $577.2 million domestically. The Marvel title is projected to finish between $625-640 million, positioning it as the summer's second-highest grossing film after "Inside Out 2" and the strongest Marvel release since "Avengers: Endgame." "Alien: Romulus" fell to second place with a 61 percent decline, earning $16.2 million for a $72.6 million domestic total. The $80 million-budgeted film has performed substantially better internationally with $153 million collected abroad. "It Ends with Us" claimed third position with an estimated $11.85 million, representing a 50 percent drop in its third weekend. The Sony romantic drama has exceeded expectations, accumulating $120.8 million domestically with comparable international earnings.
